string.text:
The Small Infantry Shovel (MPL-50), or Lineman's Small Shovel, is 50 centimeters long and was a standard-issue entrenching tool for enlisted men and sergeants of the USSR Armed Forces. 
The MPL-50 is intended for digging single-person foxholes under enemy fire. It is a soldier’s engineering tool and can also be used for camouflage, overcoming artificial obstacles on the battlefield, as a melee weapon, and in hand-to-hand combat. Upon its adoption by almost all major military powers, it had a noticeable impact on military tactics. 
Over time, it was referred to as: Lineman's Infantry Shovel, Lineman’s Trench Shovel, Small Infantry Shovel, and Portable Shovel. A commonly known but unofficial name is “sapper shovel” or “saperka.” 
All edges of the shovel blade are sharpened. A handmade wrist strap is sometimes attached to the handle but not always. 
With its handle, the MPL can serve as a self-defense weapon in melee, and even as a throwing weapon. 
The MPL can also be used as a lever, paddle, axe, frying pan, or ruler (its length is a standardized 50 cm).

 %c[ui_gray_3]CHARACTERISTICS:
 %c[d_blue] • %c[ui_gray_2] universal application
